description: For a child or other animal to be born or hatch. The arrival itself gets the verb; parenthood and the circumstances of birth belong to the surrounding clause. Plants ordinarily 'thuroa' (grow) from seed unless a speaker deliberately chooses a birth image.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let the breath pass into rounded middle-height back 'o'. Round the lips and lift the back of the tongue for 'w', then move into stressed high-front 'i'. Keep the final open central 'a' in its own syllable.
sound_symbolism: The form narrows to breath, brightens under stress, and opens once more: a small contour of arrival.
usage_notes: Use 'thowia' intransitively. By the general rule, noun use means birth, as in 'thia thowia philo' (the day of your birth). Established phrases include 'thowia phao' (birth parent) and 'wea thowia shua' (come towards birth), which points forwards without assigning intention.

semantic_domains.community: birth as the relation that connects a child with a birth parent
semantic_domains.nature: birth or hatching among living animals
semantic_domains.temporal: a dated threshold in an animal's life
